reasoning: The probability of Elon Musk ceasing to be CEO of Tesla in 2025 is very low. Initially, Manifold estimated this at 6%. A review of recent events indicates that while there have been shareholder complaints and executive departures at his companies, Musk recently publicly committed to staying at Tesla for another 5 years (May 2025). Importantly, on August 3, 2025, the Tesla board approved a $26 billion interim pay award explicitly conditional on him remaining at the company for another two years. Furthermore, Musk stepped down from his U.S. Government advisory role in late May 2025, freeing up his time to focus on Tesla and alleviating a primary source of shareholder pressure. Barring a completely unforeseeable event like a sudden severe health crisis, sudden death, or an unprecedented scandal rapidly escalating to a board ouster in the next four months, Musk is highly incentivized and likely to remain CEO. A 4% probability accounts for these tail risks over the remaining four months of 2025.
